open_findings.title: Routing API returns HTTP 200 with default config when file-backed config throws
open_findings.description: GET /api/engine/routing catches any error from readFileRoutingConfig/buildRoutingConfig, logs it, then responds with NextResponse.json(buildRoutingConfig(), { status: 200 }). Callers and caches see a successful response and cannot distinguish failure from a valid empty/default routing configuration, which can hide misconfiguration or disk/read errors in production.

proof_hooks.summary: Catch block returns 200 with default buildRoutingConfig() after logging the error.

open_findings.impact: Operators and UIs may assume routing is valid when the engine is actually using fallbacks after a failed read; debugging production routing issues becomes harder and monitoring based on HTTP status will not fire.

suggested_fix.approach: On configuration read failure, return 503 or 500 with a structured error body, or include a boolean flag such as ok:false / source:'fallback' in the JSON while still returning a safe default if the product requires a body. Align with other engine routes that surface errors.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Clients that assume 200 always means file-backed config may need to handle new status codes or response fields.
tests_needed: Add route test: mock readFileRoutingConfig to throw; assert status and payload contract.

history.notes: Derived from static review of route handler.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-707): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-707): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify

open_findings.title: Captured typecheck artifact is not a real TypeScript compile
open_findings.description: audits/artifacts/_run_/typecheck.txt contains the npm stub message 'This is not the tsc command you are looking for' instead of tsc --noEmit output. The audit batch therefore has no static type-check signal for this run, so type errors could ship without appearing in artifacts.

proof_hooks.summary: typecheck.txt shows wrong tsc stub; no project typecheck output.
Root package.json defines turbo typecheck; artifact capture did not invoke project tsc successfully.
proof_hooks.command: npm run typecheck (expected: turbo run typecheck / per-package tsc --noEmit)
proof_hooks.expected: TypeScript compiler diagnostics or empty success from tsc --noEmit
proof_hooks.actual: Stub message from mistaken global/wrong tsc on PATH

open_findings.impact: Logic and typing regressions in dashboard/worker/packages may go undetected until runtime or a later CI run.

suggested_fix.approach: Ensure the audit pipeline runs typecheck from repo root with devDependencies (e.g. npx --no-install tsc or turbo run typecheck after npm ci) and fix PATH/npx so the wrong 'tsc' package is never invoked.
tests_needed: Re-run capture; confirm typecheck.txt contains tsc output or clean exit.

history.notes: Evidence from audits/artifacts/_run_/typecheck.txt
Status synced from Linear (PLP-708): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-708): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify

open_findings.title: Deep-link useEffect omits setFilter from dependency array
open_findings.description: eslint react-hooks/exhaustive-deps flags ProjectView: the effect that auto-selects a finding and calls setFilter('all') lists [initialFindingId, findings] but not setFilter. setFilter is recreated each render (not useCallback), so the effect can theoretically see a stale setter reference in edge cases; aligning with exhaustive-deps reduces subtle React 18 strict-mode or future refactors risk.

proof_hooks.summary: Lint reported missing dependency setFilter on useEffect in ProjectView.
proof_hooks.error_text: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'setFilter'. Either include it or remove the dependency array

proof_hooks.summary: Effect calls setFilter without listing it in deps.
Lint output records warning at ProjectView.tsx line 136.

open_findings.impact: Low probability of stale filter state when combining deep links with session storage filter changes; mostly maintainability and lint compliance.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap setFilter in useCallback with stable deps, or add setFilter to the dependency array per ESLint, and verify deep-link + filter behavior in the UI.
tests_needed: Optional component test: initialFindingId + findings triggers setFilter('all') and selection.

history.notes: Matches lint.txt warning for ProjectView.tsx

Status synced from Linear (PLP-709): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify

open_findings.title: Worker audit metrics uses explicit any casts around logAuditMetrics
open_findings.description: process-job.ts uses `as any` when passing LLM metric objects to logAuditMetrics (and similar patterns elsewhere per lint). That bypasses compile-time checks: a wrong property name or shape would only fail at runtime or in downstream cost code.

proof_hooks.summary: Cast to any for AuditLlmResult-like object passed to logAuditMetrics.
ESLint reports @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any at multiple lines in process-job.ts.

open_findings.impact: Incorrect token or model fields could silently produce wrong cost accounting or failed inserts without a type error at build time.

suggested_fix.approach: Define a narrow interface matching logAuditMetrics expectations and map llm fields explicitly; remove as any.

open_findings.title: Linear sync API routes reference a table that is never created in migrations
open_findings.description: POST handlers for bulk Linear sync insert into `penny_linear_sync_new` with columns `(project_name, finding_id, linear_issue_id, linear_team_key)` and `ON CONFLICT (project_name, finding_id)`. No migration in `supabase/migrations/` defines `penny_linear_sync_new`. The only related object is `penny_linear_sync` in 20260403105735_penny_manifests_linear.sql, with a different shape (single-row-per-project `state` JSONB, PK `project_name` only). Deployed databases will raise undefined_table errors when these routes run.

open_findings.title: Dashboard server Supabase client uses the service role key, bypassing RLS
open_findings.description: `createSupabaseServerClient` builds the JS client with `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`. In Supabase, the service role bypasses row level security. Policies on `projects`, `findings`, `audit_runs`, and related tables therefore do not constrain queries from API routes that use this client. Multi-tenant isolation must rely entirely on application-level checks (middleware, explicit filters). This contradicts comments in 20260408120000_penny_tables_rls.sql that imply end-user JWT usage for dashboard routes.

proof_hooks.content: Client access: dashboard routes using createSupabaseServerClient use the end-user JWT (authenticated);
proof_hooks.summary: [code_ref] Client access: dashboard routes using createSupabaseServerClient use the end-user JWT (authenticated);

open_findings.title: Finding.confidence is a number in shared types but TEXT in the canonical SQL schema
open_findings.description: `packages/shared-types` defines `Finding.confidence` as `number` (0.0–1.0). The initial LYRA migration defines `findings.confidence` as `TEXT` with a comment suggesting enum-like strings ('evidence', 'inference', 'speculation'). Python repair API uses string confidence in places and optional numeric breakdowns. This drift can cause silent coercion bugs or invalid inserts if not normalized at boundaries.

proof_hooks.content: TS: number vs SQL: text — requires explicit mapping layer or schema change

open_findings.title: lane_* tables grant broad privileges to the anon role
open_findings.description: 20260407175738_remote_schema.sql grants DELETE, INSERT, UPDATE, SELECT, etc. on `lane_embeddings`, `lane_messages`, `lane_runs`, and `lane_sessions` to `anon`. RLS policies restrict rows to `auth.uid() = user_id`, which usually blocks unauthenticated access, but broad grants increase blast radius if policies are misconfigured or bypassed. Prefer minimal grants (e.g. authenticated-only) unless anon access is explicitly required.
open_findings.impact: See description.

open_findings.title: Ingest edge function has no caller authentication
open_findings.description: The `ingest-audit` Supabase Edge Function creates a `supabase-js` client with `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` and upserts `audit_runs`, `findings`, and `orchestration_events` from the request body. There is no verification of a shared secret, Supabase JWT, or other caller credential in the handler. Attack scenario: anyone who can invoke the deployed function URL (URL discovery, leaked project ref, or browser/CORS context if exposed) sends a crafted JSON body and overwrites or poisons audit results and findings for arbitrary `project_id` values, corrupting compliance data and downstream repair/orchestration decisions.

proof_hooks.summary: Handler uses service role and writes without auth check.

steps: Deploy or point at the ingest-audit function endpoint.
POST JSON with project_id and synthesizer_payload matching expected shape.
Observe 200 and rows inserted/updated in audit_runs/findings without any Authorization validation in code.

repro_steps: POST to the edge function URL with a valid synthesizer-shaped body.
Confirm writes succeed without presenting a secret or user JWT in application logic.
open_findings.impact: Full integrity loss for audit data tied to this pipeline: fake findings, deleted accountability, and wrong repair triggers for projects.

suggested_fix.approach: Require a server-only secret (compare timing-safe), or verify Supabase JWT with role checks, or disable public invocation and rely on Supabase function JWT verification in project config—ensure code and platform config agree.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Breaking change for any caller until they send the new credential.

history.notes: Evidence from static review of edge function source.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-732): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-732): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify

open_findings.title: Empty REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET accepts empty Bearer token
open_findings.description: `repair-callback` compares `Authorization` Bearer text to `Deno.env.get('REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET')`. If the secret is set to an empty string in deployment, `token !== expectedToken` is false when both are empty, so the handler accepts `Authorization: Bearer ` with no secret. Attack scenario: misconfiguration (empty env in Supabase secrets) leaves the callback open; an attacker invokes the function and updates arbitrary `repair_job_id` rows via the service-role client.

proof_hooks.summary: Bearer compared to env; no reject when secret missing or empty before compare.

repro_steps: Set REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET to empty in the function environment.
POST with Header Authorization: Bearer (empty token after prefix).
Observe 200 and repair_jobs update if id exists.
open_findings.impact: Unauthorized updates to repair job status, PR metadata, and orchestration_events linkage.

suggested_fix.approach: Reject early if `!expectedToken?.trim()` with 503; use timing-safe compare for secrets.

history.notes: Logic review of callback auth branch.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-733): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-733): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify

open_findings.title: penny_ALLOW_OPEN_API disables dashboard API authentication
open_findings.description: When neither `DASHBOARD_API_SECRET` nor `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET` is set, production allows all `/api/*` traffic if `penny_ALLOW_OPEN_API` is `1` or `true`. `proxy.ts` logs a warning but still calls `next()`. Attack scenario: operator mistake or compromised env leaves the dashboard API fully open; any client can read or mutate project state exposed by those routes with no shared secret or user session.

proof_hooks.summary: Open API branch returns next() for all API routes.
Flag enables open API in production when no secrets.

repro_steps: Unset DASHBOARD_API_SECRET and SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET in a production-like build.
Call any /api/* route without Authorization and observe success.
open_findings.impact: Unauthorized access to dashboard-backed data and actions (project CRUD, orchestration, imports) depending on route behavior.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove or gate `penny_ALLOW_OPEN_API` behind explicit local-only hostname; fail closed in production regardless of flag; alert on startup.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: May break intentional staging setups; document migration.

history.notes: Documented intentional DX escape hatch with production hazard.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-734): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-734): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify

open_findings.title: Bearer secrets compared with plain equality
open_findings.description: Repair service `require_auth` and the repair-callback use `!=` / `!==` for bearer tokens. Attack scenario: local timing side channels are usually low risk on cloud runtimes, but network attackers with very fine-grained timing to the same instance could theoretically learn bytes; primary practical concern is consistency with crypto best practices for long-lived shared secrets.
open_findings.impact: Marginal risk of secret byte leakage via timing; defense-in-depth gap versus constant-time compare.

suggested_fix.approach: Use timing-safe comparison (e.g. `hmac.compare_digest` in Python; SubtleCrypto or hex compare loop in Deno) after normalizing encoding once.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: None significant if only fixing comparison.

history.notes: Pairing Python and Deno bearer checks.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-735): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-735): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify

open_findings.title: CI does not build or test the Python repair service
open_findings.description: GitHub Actions runs `pnpm lint`, `typecheck`, `test`, and `build` for the pnpm/Turbo workspaces only. `services/repair` is deployed via Docker on Railway (`services/repair/railway.toml`, `services/repair/Dockerfile`) but has no CI step to `docker build` or run Python tests. Broken `requirements.txt` or Dockerfile changes can reach production without failing PR checks.

proof_hooks.summary: CI job only runs pnpm commands; no repair service step.
Repair image built from Dockerfile on Railway.

open_findings.impact: Regressions in the repair API image or dependencies may ship undetected until deploy or runtime failure.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a CI job (or step) that builds `services/repair` with Docker and optionally runs `pytest` or `python -m compileall` if tests exist.
tests_needed: CI green on a PR that only touches services/repair/

open_findings.title: Lint reports many warnings while CI still passes
open_findings.description: Latest captured `pnpm lint` output shows 17 worker warnings and 19 dashboard warnings (unused symbols, `no-explicit-any`, hooks deps). ESLint exits zero on warnings, so CI does not block merges on this debt.

proof_hooks.summary: Lint log shows 17 + 19 problems, all warnings.

proof_hooks.expected: Optional: fail on warnings if policy requires

open_findings.impact: Unused code and `any` types accumulate; harder refactors and more runtime risk in hot paths over time.

suggested_fix.approach: Triage warnings, fix or prefix unused bindings with `_`, replace `any` incrementally; optionally add `--max-warnings 0` to lint scripts or CI once the backlog is cleared.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Turning warnings into errors will fail CI until the backlog is addressed.

history.notes: Evidence from audits/artifacts/_run_/lint.txt

Status synced from Linear (PLP-737): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify

open_findings.title: No single startup validation for all required environment variables
open_findings.description: `.env.example` documents many variables. Some paths validate configuration when used (for example `readDatabaseConfig` in `lib/postgres.ts`, and `proxy.ts` guards API auth), but there is no one central schema (for example Zod or explicit checks at process start) that fails fast in every deploy context if critical vars are missing or malformed.

proof_hooks.summary: Large template without a single validating entrypoint.
Middleware returns 503 when secrets missing only for protected API paths logic.

open_findings.impact: Misconfigured production deploys may partially work or fail late on specific routes instead of at boot with a clear list of missing keys.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a small env schema module validated once when the server starts (or in `instrumentation.ts` if used), listing required vars per deployment profile.
tests_needed: Unit test: missing required env produces deterministic startup error

history.notes: Inferred from distributed validation vs centralized schema.
